transcript.text: We're going to stick with this until probably January.
I don't know.
It is everyone's favorite season, so yeah.
This meeting is being recorded.
Hey Theo, how are you?
Yeah, I'm fine.
Hi, everyone.
Hi, Theo.
Let me share my screen.
We'll get it started.
All right, in terms of new content updates, we delivered seven last week and seven scheduled for this week.
Just as a sort of announcement, we will be delivering articles until the week of Christmas.
So even that week, we will be delivering.
It's just that the final week of the year, we have kind of a quiet week.
So we'll still be around.
You on Slack just to check for any notifications or any needs that you might have, but that week we're kind of, we won't be publishing anything.
So yeah, in terms of outstanding issues, we're pretty much good everywhere.
So going to the comparison page, I had sent that list over of the top performers for Notum to sort of update and delete the big table.
I don't know if Paul's on call, but was wondering if there was any updates over there?
I can share an update.
We will be removing the table.
So it's with the agency.
They are adding basically a toggle in the back end to basically be able to choose on what page to display the table or not.
So yeah.
Cool.
So yeah, I'll let you know as soon as it's done.
Cool.
Then moving on.
So this one has been open for a while.
So it was about reviewing the comparison pages and refreshing the videos, et cetera.
I had left this on Theo's Play, but I'm not sure it was Theo or Paul.
So I just wanted to flag that we had sent the list of pages to update.
I was wondering if you need any more context here?
at all?
Or is it just something that needs to be done?
Yeah, I could still check it out and give you some feedback.
Cool.
All right.
And then regarding the FAQ components, so the capabilities and features pages are done.
We're just fixing or updating the integrations pages.
So the issues there were either the FAQ was too long and it was just blowing up the element good thing.
So the FAQ
We had a bit of an internal issue where some of the FAQs we generated were not quite FAQs, so it was just a small fix.
The ETF for this is Friday, and things are on track.
And then the last item was just reviewing the GrowthX generated integration pages for a potential refresh.
We started this, and I will have a report by the end of this week, slash update.
Generally speaking, there's not much potential for these pages.
Any updates would really just be smaller updates just to indicate that we are refreshing them.
But content-wise, they're pretty much there.
So it's more a question of just looking at, you know, what is the competition doing?
Is it a case of the search volume itself being low, or is it just a case of competitors ranking ahead of us?
For most pages, that's not the case.
It's not like we've been jumped ahead by other comparison or integration pages, for example.
So...

So, so far I'm leaning towards just the search volumes being a little low, and I had another question about this page, so we had spoken about replacing the FAQ page with sort of an experimental LLM, about, strapi kind of a page, we had a question, so we updated the page in the CMS in the backend, and we had formatted it for, you know, markdown H2s, etc.
But on this page, you can see how it's displaying.
I was wondering if you could just help us troubleshoot, is there something we need to do here, or something we're doing here, Yeah, I could check it out, and see what is possibly wrong.
Okay, cool.
And I will add that to the last list after the meeting.
So that's pretty much the updates for all the existing issues.
We are at the month end, so a bit of a deeper dive into performance over the past month.
Overall sessions and engaged sessions increased, as you can see, despite there was a bit of a slogan during the week of Thanksgiving, but despite that we outpaced last month as well.

Another positive sign was non-branded impressions also increased month on month, so we're not close to that massive spike that happened around March, April, but still if you sort of treat that as an anomaly, generally non-branded impressions have been along the right trend.
So it's pretty good news there.
These were the top ten pages for this past month, and pretty much all of them are from the blog.
Some of these are blogs that were published a bit more recently, so that's a pretty good sign.
Not all good news in terms of converted events.
We did see a decline in the overall number of events triggered month on month.
It wasn't a massive decline, but there was
was a decline in terms of overall events being triggered.
Within HubSpot as well, we saw drops, so the number of influence contacts, there was a slight drop.
The bigger drop was in the new contacts number, which is kind of significant at this point.
Not quite sure why that happened, we're still digging into it.
One reasoning is maybe that we're showing up a lot for informational searches and not enough for maybe more bottom-funnel content.
Maybe we need to increase the sort of the amount of content we're creating deeper in the funnel.
So that's something we're looking at and just digging into.
In terms of cohort performance, generally cohort performance, it followed the same trend as overall sessions.
It was pretty good.
We maintained positions, not much volatility there.
As I mentioned, Thanksgiving week, we saw a bit of a traffic blip, which was expected, but it wasn't a massive.
It didn't pull down the number of sessions overall.
The GrowthX integration pages, as we kind of saw about two, three weeks back as well, they lost the most sessions month-on-month.
We're looking at potential enhancements, but really the thinking here is that the overall search volume, or TAM, if you will, is a bit low.
But still we're looking into it to see if we can make any improvements.
In terms of LLM traffic, referrals slowed down a bit.
Most of this was due to just Thanksgiving week being extremely slow, in terms of LLM queries.
Scrunch data shows the same positive trends.
We are well ahead of Contentful and Sanity, etc., across all the queries that we're tracking.
In terms of ranking across categories as well, we haven't really lost any major positions.
So, generally, things are looking quite good overall overall in terms of performance.
performance.
So, So,
I'm sure there are questions.
Anything I can answer about any of this?
Yeah, I got a question about the integration pages.
I think we've added a couple to Airtable.
I guess the priority on integration was adding the FAQs.
When will we get back to adding integration pages to it?
We're actually generating those.
So I think we had the new integration pages that should be going out this week and next week.
So that's not being held back due to the FAQ update like they're running in Parallel.
So they should be coming out soon.
Okay.
Sounds good.
And yeah, I guess just an update for you.
We're fixing the cross-linking on those pages.
I think that might help as well, too.
you.

Yeah, something I wanted to flag was that there are a number of articles under review, like that queue is building up a bit, and there are some comparisons and I think even some of the newer integration pages stuck in that queue, so yeah, if you could take a look at it when you have the time and, you know, give us some feedback that we can start clearing that into All right, just wanted to check as well in terms of Q1 priorities for next year, is there, you know, just need as much information as possible, is there any particular area you'd like to focus on, maybe so far we've been targeting mostly the developer persona, is there any appetite at all?
To expand to maybe content teams and that sort of less technical persona, just wanted to get your thoughts on that and, yeah, get some pointers there.
Well, if anything, we'll be doubling down on the devolver persona.
So that's really the focus.
We want to, you know, keep the focus as well on, like, fewer but better bits of content in general.
So I think, like, the volume, if the volume can come from growthx, I think that's great.
So I think continuing with the integration pages, think there is a number of maybe identifying the new batch of up-and-coming technologies that are worth adding to our directory.
I think that there would be time well-spent.
so...
One initiative that we have in mind for next year is to work with those third-party technology to get backlinks.
So, for instance, we worked with the Expo team, and they were able to add a call-out in their documentation that links back to the Expo integration page.
Same thing with Resend.
Zio worked with the Resend team to get it promoted.
And so I think that's really the muscle we want to build.
It's like creating new integration pages and then working with those companies to get backlinks and kind of reference that as a useful piece of content.
So I think there's definitely appetite on developer integrations.
And, yeah, I think Paul is not in the meeting, right?
But it would be good for him to share a bit more information on.
Okay.
Another initiative he plans to work on, which is a codename Strapify.
It might not be the official name, but the idea with Strapify is to identify some popular templates and adding Strapi as the backend to that template.

So maybe there is a collaboration there on, you know, like he, they can, maybe Paul can create the code piece and then growthx can deliver on the, you know, the, the blog post, the associated blog post.
I don't really know.
So just, yeah, it's still, you know, being discussed and brainstormed as we planned for 2026, but that's another initiative that we have in mind.
Yeah, and maybe another, another one is around agencies.
So we still see agencies waiting.

I think we have good content on that overall, but I think we could potentially try to see from a query standpoint, maybe do a little bit of research on what are some of queries specifically for agencies and how maybe we can rank for that.
So, again, not that super structured, but that's where our heads are at at the moment.
Just a question about the agencies.
Is that persona information in Octave?
Or is there somewhere we can get that data?
Because then that will help us do the research.
I'd have to double check.
I think it is, but I'd have to double check.
Okay.
We'll check as well on our end and I'll follow up on that.
Yeah, that would be great.
So, yes, I think we have some content, so I think maybe we can review the list of what we have and see where the gaps are.
But, yeah, and after that, like the, I think there is, you know, what we discussed a few weeks back around the social listening piece.

I think that's another topic where we really want to invest is, you know, getting ahead of, you know, burning questions on popular subreddits and trying to get timely when it comes to covering new releases.
For instance, like, you know, NextGS16 was just released, like, it would be nice to put some content out that is covering that new release.
So, yeah, that's another kind of angle, like, the social listening and keeping a pulse.
On like the, what's hype at the moment in terms of technology and releases?
Sure, yeah.
I'll keep following up on that internally.
Like I mentioned, for now, it's, yeah, we're kind of monitoring Reddit, etc.
So we're getting some signals from there, nothing major as yet, but, you know, it looks promising in terms of just giving us a good recap of what's going on.
But, yes, social listening definitely be noted in terms of priority.
And, yeah, so the other reason, I think, as Mara previously mentioned, was that the plan is to sort of open Atlas to customers in Q1, towards the end of Q1.
So there is some thinking that, okay, can customers even maybe customize it, etc.
But, you know, just letting you know that that's on the horizon, too.
So I'll follow up with that, and I'll definitely give an update.
you.
When, when we have one.
Okay.
All right.
That sounds good.
And, yeah, and same thing with, like, the, the Kappa, Kappa integration, for, like, the content gaps.
think that's something we also look forward to.
So, is that still held up until, until, like, your Atlas becomes open?
Am I hearing?
It is.
Yeah, okay.
Yeah, unfortunately, it is.
The, the thing that's, the reason it's being held up, so we're kind of, I'm kind of folding this into broader social listening.
The, the reason it's being held up is because of the tool specificity.
So, the engineering team right now is prioritizing building workflows that can be reusable across multiple clients.
Since we're using Kappa or Octolens specific tools, you know, the, the engineering data strip is more like, okay, this is tool specific.
don't So, I
It's not going to carry over.
So that's why, unfortunately, it's not coming to the top.
But yeah, as I said, I mean, you know, this is something that even I want.
So I'm just trying to push it as much as possible and yeah, definitely fighting for it internally.
I'll give you updates for sure when we have something there.
That sounds good.
Yeah, just to add, I would love if you would like have those topics or reviews on Slack.
I mean, I would be glad if you tagged me so I keep it to my to-do so that I don't miss out on it.
Yeah.
Sure.
Thank you.
Would you prefer if we just drop links to Airtable?
Yeah, yeah, yeah, exactly.
I think that's the way we've been doing it.
You just like give me the name, like what I'm doing and then the link to them.
I am Paul.
If it's something I could do myself, I could just do it.
Okay, no problem.
We can do that on a weekly basis.
So after our syncs, I could just drop in like post call, action takeaways or whatever.
Just leave money for review and I'll tag you.
I'll you on the phone.
Yeah, thank you.
Great.
I think we can even automate that.
I'll see if we can automate it.
I don't want to break Airtable, so that's the reason I'm not experimenting.
But yeah, we can certainly look at it.
Great.
Cool.
That was pretty much it.
I'll follow up with all of these action items and get started on that.
Hey, Vivek, I got one more question.
How is the image workflow performing?
I haven't been reviewing the latest article, but are we, just out of curiosity, how many images are we able to include in those posts?
And like, do we...
Maybe, like, I'm trying to see if they're actually, like, making a difference.
And so maybe we can give you access to Hotjar so you can actually see, like, if, you know, they'll show up on, like, heat maps or something like that.
But, yeah, what's your, what's the latest on images?
So we enhanced, so we had an enhanced image workflow rollout, I think, of two to three weeks back.
And we have been including them in the latest batch of articles since then.
The image quality is pretty, I would say, good.
We're still doing a lot of infographic type images because that makes sense for Strapi.
We're using basically a Figma template as a background that, you know, we had taken from your existing brand assets.
So image quality generally is looking pretty good from an internal review.
And I think when Theo reviews some of the latest articles, he can offer more feedback.

In terms of looking at heat maps, for sure, if you can give us access, we can certainly take a look and see, like, what kind of an effect they're having on content, or at least how people are interacting with them.
That would be great.
Awesome.
Sounds good.
All right.
Thanks, everyone.
I'm sorry, go on.
Thanks a lot.
Go ahead, Victor.
No, I was going to say, have a good day, but do you have one more question?
Yeah, yeah.
About the images.
So, I have been seeing them in a few blog posts, but I think it would be great if we have them around.
And so far, it's only been one per post, although not all the posts.
I think it would be great if we have, like, some image for any blog posts, yeah.
And so far, they look good.
So, yeah.
And so, know, because I've ever happened
They are preferable compared to the ones we saw in the past, and they are very technical as well, so yeah.
Yeah, what we did with these newer sort of flows, basically, we're kind of using text, highlighting text within the content and generating something based on that.
So, point noted, I will ask Usman to add more images in the article.
All right.
Cool.
Thank you for that.
All right.
Thanks, team.
Have a good day.
Thanks, everyone.
Bye.
Thank you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review monthly performance and align on Q1 2026 content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Performance:** Sessions and non-branded impressions grew MoM, but new contact acquisition dropped significantly. The cause is under investigation, with a leading theory of over-indexing on informational content.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [**Q1 Strategy:** The focus is doubling down on the developer persona with a new strategy: create integration pages for emerging tech and partner with those companies for backlinks in their official docs.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:** 7 articles were delivered last week, with 7 more scheduled for this week. Publishing continues until Christmas week, with a quiet period the final week of the year.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=35.0)
  - [**Process Improvement:** To clear a growing review queue, Vivek will tag Theo on Slack with Airtable links for all pending content reviews.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1043.0)

## Topics

### Monthly Performance Review (Nov)

  - [**Traffic & Impressions:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [Sessions and engaged sessions increased MoM.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
      - [Non-branded impressions also increased, maintaining a positive trend.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=317.0)
      - [Top 10 pages are all blog posts, including recent publications.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=334.0)
  - [**Conversions & Contacts:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [Overall converted events declined MoM.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=349.0)
      - [A significant drop in new contact acquisition was observed in HubSpot.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=362.0)
      - [**Hypothesis:** Over-indexing on informational content → attracting top-funnel users.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=370.0)
      - [**Action:** Investigate the cause and consider creating more bottom-funnel content.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=370.0)
  - [**Cohort & Page Performance:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)
      - [Cohort performance remained stable, with no major ranking volatility.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=402.0)
      - [GrowthX-generated integration pages lost the most sessions MoM.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=423.0)
      - [**Hypothesis:** Low total addressable market (TAM) for these specific integrations.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=423.0)
      - [**Action:** A refresh report is due this week to confirm this.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=192.0)
  - [**Competitive Landscape:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=456.0)
      - [Scrunch data shows Strapi remains well ahead of Contentful and Sanity across all tracked queries.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=456.0)
      - [No major ranking positions were lost in key categories.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=467.0)

### Content Pipeline & Issue Resolution

  - [**Comparison Page Table:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=76.0)
      - [The large table will be removed.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=76.0)
      - [The agency is building a backend toggle to control its display on a page-by-page basis.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=76.0)
  - [**Comparison Page Videos:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=126.0)
      - [Theo will review the list of pages needing video refreshes.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=126.0)
  - [**FAQ Components:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
      - [Capabilities & Features pages are complete.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
      - [Integrations pages are being fixed (resolving layout issues from long FAQs). **ETA: Friday.**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=164.0)
  - [**Integration Pages:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
      - [New pages are being generated in parallel with the FAQ updates and will be published this week and next.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
      - [Internal cross-linking is also being fixed to improve SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=528.0)
  - [**LLM "About Strapi" Page:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)
      - [The page is not rendering markdown formatting (e.g., H2s) correctly.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)
      - [Theo will investigate the cause.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=239.0)

### Q1 2026 Content Strategy

  - [**Core Focus:** Double down on the developer persona with a "fewer but better" content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=590.0)
  - [**New Initiatives:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [**Backlink Generation:** Create integration pages for emerging tech and partner with those companies for backlinks in their official docs.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
          - [**Model:** Successful partnerships with Expo and Resend.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [**"Strapify" (Codename):** Identify popular templates and add Strapi as the backend.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
          - [**Collaboration:** Paul (code) + GrowthX (blog post).](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=720.0)
      - [**Agency Persona:** Research agency-specific queries to fill content gaps.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=770.0)
          - [**Action:** Vivek to check Octave for agency persona data.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=816.0)
      - [**Social Listening:** Create timely content on new releases (e.g., Next.js 16) and answer questions on subreddits.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=852.0)
  - [**Blocker:** The Kappa/Octolens content gap integration is on hold.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=966.0)
      - [**Reason:** Engineering is prioritizing reusable, tool-agnostic workflows.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=990.0)
      - [**Timeline:** Likely unblocked when Atlas opens to customers in late Q1.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=966.0)

### Image Workflow & Feedback

  - [**Process:** Enhanced workflow uses a Figma template and generates images based on highlighted text in the content.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1161.0)
  - [**Feedback:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1121.0)
      - [**Quality:** Images are a significant improvement and are highly technical.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1234.0)
      - [**Quantity:** Only one image per post is being included.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1234.0)
  - [**Action:** Vivek will ask Usman to increase the number of images per article.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1234.0)
  - [**Tracking:** Victor will grant GrowthX Hotjar access to analyze user interaction with the new images via heatmaps.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1140.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Vivek:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1043.0)
      - [Tag Theo on Slack with Airtable links for all pending content reviews.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=1043.0)
      - [Deliver the integration page refresh report by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=192.0)
      - [Investigate the cause of the new contact acquisition drop.](https://fathom.video/share/Cd59vUe8bhqNubMVTJgVBxLRq6216Y9c?tab=summary&timestamp=370.0)
